rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND MATTHEWS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) the sole asset of the estate is a 50% tenancy-in-common interest in real property, but the inventory and appraisal shows the 100% interest. In a verified supplement, please indicate why no corrected inventory and appraisal has been filed. 2) The petition for letters of administration in this estate indicates that the decedent was survived by his sister, Debra Matthews, but the instant petition requests distribution to the Estate of Henri Matthews, the decedent's father. If not all heirs were known and disclosed at the outset of the proceedings, the administrator must file a petition to determine persons entitled to distribution prior to petitioning for any distribution (Probate Code Section 11700 and San Francisco Local Rule 14.35.H). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 10/01/2025 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW SHUEN CHARK LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No proof of service of the complete notice of the petition with remote instructions attached has been filed. No proof of service of notice of the amendment has been filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] Probate petitions require the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, to which the instructions for how to appear remotely must be attached. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. (An examiner does not request compliance; examiners merely point out defects if a petition fails to meet statutory or procedural requirements. An amendment or a supplement to a petition should stand on its own and not quote or cite issues posted by an examiner.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARLOS NELSON MONTOYA JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A Court-appointed fiduciary must supplement or amend her own pleading(s), not the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c)] (2) A petition is either supplemented (to provide additional information) or amended (to correct allegations), not by responding to issues posted by an examiner via a declaration. [CRC 7.3.] The attorney's "Declaration" e-filed on 8/25/25 AMENDED the petition. (3) An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (4) Most of the liabilities ($6,678.60) do not appear to have been accounted for as receipts, so they do not have to be deducted from the fee base. Only the $1,550.00 cash advance already reimbursed to the administrator and the $123.00 refund from the FTB (for taxes paid by the estate) should be deducted from the fee base. (5) On the other hand, the amount paid to a third party to prepare the accounting ($1,500.00) still has not been deducted from the personal representative's statutory compensation. (6) What Court Admin fee? The court's register of actions does not reflect these. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/1/25. [Issues should be addressed in a SUPPLEMENT or an AMENDMENT that just addresses the issues, not as answers to issues posted by an examiner, which are not part of the record, in any event.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE JEAN PFERDEHIRT LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of First, Second, And Third Accounts And Reports Of Trustee Prob Code 17200 B 5
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) This Court will not review/approve three accountings in one petition. The first accounting for no more than two-years must first be adjudicated before subsequent accountings can be filed, reviewed, and approved. [LRSF 14.34 A.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) This is not a Court-supervised trust. Please file statements or appraisals supporting the starting values. (4) Under what authority did the successor begin to serve prior to the settlor's death? Paragraph 3 of the petition is rather vague. Did the settlor resign in writing or was she deemed to be incapacitated (Article XII of the trust)? (5) How were the legal fees determined? Were there orders authorizing them in the estate proceedings? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/15/25. If Court APPROVAL is sought, the first accounting should be filed separately in an amended petition, or, if there is sufficient proof that the successor trustee was authorized to act as of 3/29/21, and the other issues above are addressed, the Court could consider the first accounting portion of this petition without adjudicating the second and third accountings, which would have to be filed subsequently. Alternatively, if there are no objections to this petition, after these issues are addressed, the Court could simply allow, but not approve, the accountings, as filed. An appearance would be required in the latter instance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
